symptoms.character: severe, constant, hot twisting; localized to the left iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constant, localized LLQ pain is the cardinal feature of left-sided diverticulitis.
Renal colic is classically colicky/loin-to-groin rather than constant and iliac-fossa localized.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with chills and rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever with rigors indicates significant pericolic infection / systemic inflammatory response.
Rising fever and systemic upset can herald progression to a perforated/septic abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstipation — no bowel motion for ~4 days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localized inflammation causes ileus/altered bowel habit; obstipation reflects colonic dysmotility.
New change in bowel habit keeps obstructing malignancy in view as an important-not-to-miss alternative.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea, anorexia and inability to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic upset and ileus; inability to tolerate oral intake is a marker of severity that mandates IV therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known diverticulosis of the colon (risk factor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-existing diverticulosis is the substrate for diverticulitis; its presence markedly raises pre-test probability.
Absence of bloody diarrhoea argues against acute colonic ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localized guarding and positive rebound tenderness in the left iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localized peritonism in the LIF is the key examination sign of left-sided diverticulitis with peritoneal irritation.
Distinguishing localized from generalized peritonism requires hands-on examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypoactive bowel sounds with mild abdominal distension (clinician-elicited)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localized ileus from pericolic inflammation; requires auscultation and palpation unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting tachycardia (HR ~105) with fever on clinician vitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective systemic inflammatory response; the AI Doctor infers systemic upset from reported fever/rigors, not from a measured heart rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pulsatile abdominal mass (clinician-elicited pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a pulsatile mass reduces (but via telehealth cannot exclude) a vascular catastrophe as a mimic of the acute abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pericolic fat stranding on contrast CT abdomen/pelvis

investigation_details.expected_result: Pericolic fat stranding in the left/sigmoid colon +/- localized collection, confirming and grading acute diverticulitis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Complications (abscess, perforation) missed; disease severity ungraded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT pericolic fat stranding is the confirmatory imaging finding; obtainable only in hospital, sealed as answer key.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Classic acute diverticulitis triad with localized peritonism
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe localized LLQ pain + fever/rigors + altered bowel habit + LIF peritonism on known diverticulosis is the classic constellation of acute left-sided diverticulitis with peritoneal irritation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diverticulitis, grades severity (Hinchey) and identifies abscess or perforation that change management; obtained in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory bloods (FBC, CRP), lactate, renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify the systemic inflammatory response and guide IV therapy; unavailable via telehealth.
Helps distinguish urinary tract causes (pyelonephritis, renal colic) from a colonic source.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient colonoscopy after the acute episode settles
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recommended weeks after resolution to exclude colorectal malignancy underlying the new change in bowel habit; not to be performed acutely.
